summary: Plan-only PR inventory classification shard. No shared canonical was selected because the job explicitly says this is not a dedupe cluster. No GitHub mutations, merge recommendations, fix PRs, or close actions are planned.

actions.reason: Independent feature/fix PR with proof signal; no duplicate, superseding canonical, or close basis in this inventory shard.
Independent Slack message-delivery fix with passing evidence; no duplicate or low-signal close basis.
Independent narrow Codex diagnostics PR; current failing dependency-guard means it should stay open rather than be closed or merged by this plan.
Independent performance PR awaiting normal maintainer/author flow; no closure basis in this cluster.
Independent maintainer PR with focused diff and passing checks; merge is blocked by job frontmatter, so keep open.
Independent focused agents PR with proof; no duplicate, superseded, or low-signal close basis.
Independent browser compatibility PR needing proof/check follow-up; failing proof blocks merge or fixed-by-candidate closeout but does not justify closure.
Independent maintainer PR awaiting normal author/maintainer flow; no closure basis.
Independent message-delivery PR with unresolved check/review-bot evidence; keep open for normal repair/review rather than close or merge.
Independent documentation PR; low-signal closeout is explicitly disabled, so keep open.
Independent CLI PR with substantial tests and proof; no duplicate or closure basis.
Independent draft maintainer PR with dirty merge state; keep open and do not close or merge.
Independent draft provider PR awaiting normal author/maintainer work; no closure basis.
Independent subagent/session-state PR needing proof and review; no duplicate or closure basis.
Independent UI fix PR; possible merge-readiness cannot be recommended without required checks/review preflight and merge is blocked by job mode.
Independent QQBot media PR with security-boundary merge risk but no vulnerability signal; keep open for normal maintainer review and failing-check follow-up.
